_meta.subject: math
_meta.primary_source: 19 TAC Chapter 111 (Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills for Mathematics)
_meta.source_status: TEA gateway URL accessible, but direct chapter URLs require navigating subnavigation. TAC site at texreg.sos.state.tx.us has migrated. Standards below are CLAUDE-SYNTHESIZED from training knowledge of publicly-published 19 TAC Chapter 111. VERIFY against the official TAC text at https://texreg.sos.state.tx.us before relying on this file for high-stakes content (e.g., a published curriculum, an audit, or compliance documentation).
_meta.provenance_marker: [CLAUDE-SYNTHESIZED from training knowledge of publicly-published TEKS standards. Wording is paraphrased; the standard IDs and intent are accurate; the exact text should be verified against TAC.]

cognitive_demand_taxonomy.low: Verbs like identify, recall, list, name, define. Single-step.
cognitive_demand_taxonomy.medium: Verbs like compare, classify, solve, represent, apply. 1-2 step.
cognitive_demand_taxonomy.high: Verbs like analyze, evaluate, justify, generalize, prove. Multi-step or transfer.

_meta.typical_question_shape_provenance: Synthesized from observation of publicly-released STAAR forms. See state-packs/texas/test-fidelity/staar-question-shapes.md for the full pattern catalog.

standards.text: Compose and decompose numbers up to 100,000 as a sum of so many ten thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones using objects, pictorial models, and numbers, including expanded notation.
standards.typical_question_shape: Place-value question with 4-5 digit number; choices are different decompositions

standards.text: Describe the mathematical relationships found in the base-10 place value system through the hundred thousands place.
standards.typical_question_shape: Compare the value of a digit in two different positions of the same number

standards.text: Represent a number on a number line as being between two consecutive multiples of 10, 100, 1,000, or 10,000 and use words to describe relative size.
standards.typical_question_shape: Number-line stimulus with marks; identify which interval a given number falls in

standards.text: Compare and order whole numbers up to 100,000 and represent comparisons using <, >, or =.
standards.typical_question_shape: Three or four numbers; pick the correctly-ordered set

standards.text: Represent fractions greater than zero and less than or equal to one with denominators of 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8 using concrete objects and pictorial models, including strip diagrams and number lines.
standards.typical_question_shape: Shaded model of a fraction; pick which fraction is shown

standards.text: Determine the corresponding fraction greater than zero and less than or equal to one with denominators of 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8 given a specified point on a number line.
standards.typical_question_shape: Number line with marked point; identify the fraction

standards.text: Represent equivalent fractions with denominators of 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8 using a variety of objects and pictorial models, including number lines.
standards.typical_question_shape: Shaded model; pick the equivalent fraction (e.g., 1/2 and 2/4)

standards.text: Explain that two fractions are equivalent if and only if they are both represented by the same point on the number line or represent the same portion of a same-sized whole.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two number-line points or two pictorial models; identify equivalence

standards.text: Compare two fractions having the same numerator or denominator in problems by reasoning about their sizes and justifying the conclusion using symbols, words, objects, and pictorial models.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two fractions with same numerator OR same denominator; pick larger/smaller

standards.text: Solve with fluency one-step and two-step problems involving addition and subtraction within 1,000 using strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem requiring 1-2 step add/subtract within 1,000; numeric or 4-choice MC

standards.text: Determine the total number of objects when equally-sized groups of objects are combined or arranged in arrays up to 10 by 10.
standards.typical_question_shape: Array image; count total. Or word problem: N groups of M, find total

standards.text: Represent multiplication facts by using a variety of approaches such as repeated addition, equal-sized groups, arrays, area models, equal jumps on a number line, and skip counting.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multiplication fact represented by a strip-diagram, array, or jump pattern; pick the matching fact

standards.text: Recall facts to multiply up to 10 by 10 with automaticity and recall the corresponding division facts.
standards.typical_question_shape: Direct multiplication or division fact; numeric answer

standards.text: Use strategies and algorithms, including the standard algorithm, to multiply a two-digit number by a one-digit number. Strategies may include mental math, partial products, and the commutative, associative, and distributive properties.
standards.typical_question_shape: 2-digit × 1-digit multiplication; word problem or pure computation

standards.text: Determine the number of objects in each group when a set of objects is partitioned into equal shares or a set of objects is shared equally.
standards.typical_question_shape: Partition problem (sharing); 'how many in each group' word problem

standards.text: Solve one-step and two-step problems involving multiplication and division within 100 using strategies based on objects; pictorial models, including arrays, area models, and equal groups; properties of operations; or recall of facts.
standards.typical_question_shape: 1-2 step multiplication/division word problem

standards.text: Represent one- and two-step problems involving addition and subtraction of whole numbers to 1,000 using pictorial models, number lines, and equations.
standards.typical_question_shape: Bar/strip diagram; pick the matching equation OR vice versa

standards.text: Represent and solve one- and two-step multiplication and division problems within 100 using arrays, strip diagrams, and equations.
standards.typical_question_shape: Array or strip diagram given; identify equation or solve for missing value

standards.text: Represent real-world relationships using number pairs in a table and verbal descriptions.
standards.typical_question_shape: Input-output table; identify rule or fill missing entry

standards.text: Classify and sort two- and three-dimensional figures, including cones, cylinders, spheres, triangular and rectangular prisms, and cubes, based on attributes using formal geometric language.
standards.typical_question_shape: Image of solid; pick correct name

standards.text: Determine the area of rectangles with whole-number side lengths in problems using multiplication related to the number of rows times the number of unit squares in each row.
standards.typical_question_shape: Rectangle dimensions given; find area

standards.text: Decompose two congruent two-dimensional figures into parts with equal areas and express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.
standards.typical_question_shape: Shape divided into N equal parts; identify what fraction one part represents

standards.text: Determine the perimeter of a polygon or a missing length when given perimeter and remaining side lengths in problems.
standards.typical_question_shape: Polygon with side lengths shown; find perimeter, OR perimeter given and one side missing

standards.text: Determine the solutions to problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes using pictorial models or tools such as a 15-minute event plus a 30-minute event equals 45 minutes.
standards.typical_question_shape: Elapsed time word problem with start time + interval(s)

standards.text: Determine when it is appropriate to use measurements of liquid volume (capacity) or weight.
standards.typical_question_shape: Real-world scenario; pick which measurement type fits

standards.text: Determine liquid volume (capacity) or weight using appropriate units and tools.
standards.typical_question_shape: Tool image (scale, measuring cup) with reading; identify amount

standards.text: Summarize a data set with multiple categories using a frequency table, dot plot, pictograph, or bar graph with scaled intervals.
standards.typical_question_shape: Bar graph or pictograph; read off a value or sum/compare categories

standards.text: Solve one- and two-step problems using categorical data represented with a frequency table, dot plot, pictograph, or bar graph with scaled intervals.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two-step graph problem (e.g., difference between categories)

standards.text: Explain the connection between human capital/labor and income.
standards.typical_question_shape: Scenario about a job; pick how income is earned

standards.text: Identify the costs and benefits of planned and unplanned spending decisions.
standards.typical_question_shape: Scenario; identify cost or benefit of a decision

standards.text: Explain that credit is used when wants or needs exceed the ability to pay and that it is the borrower's responsibility to pay it back to the lender, usually with interest.
standards.typical_question_shape: Scenario about borrowing; identify what credit is or what 'interest' means

standards.text: Interpret the value of each place-value position as 10 times the position to the right and as one-tenth of the value of the place to its left.
standards.typical_question_shape: Compare two place-value positions in same number

standards.text: Represent the value of the digit in whole numbers through 1,000,000,000 and decimals to the hundredths using expanded notation and numerals.
standards.typical_question_shape: Large multi-digit number; identify the value of a specific digit (e.g., '5' in 5,127,304). CAUTION: per CLAUDE.md §13, ensure the chosen digit appears at exactly one position; otherwise AMBIGUITY.

standards.text: Compare and order whole numbers to 1,000,000,000 and represent comparisons using <, >, or =.
standards.typical_question_shape: Three or four large numbers; pick correct ordering

standards.text: Represent decimals, including tenths and hundredths, using concrete and visual models and money.
standards.typical_question_shape: Decimal grid (10x10 grid shaded); identify decimal

standards.text: Compare and order decimals using concrete and visual models to the hundredths.
standards.typical_question_shape: Decimals to hundredths; pick correct ordering

standards.text: Relate decimals to fractions that name tenths and hundredths.
standards.typical_question_shape: Equivalent decimal/fraction pair; pick the match

standards.text: Determine the corresponding decimal to the tenths or hundredths place of a specified point on a number line.
standards.typical_question_shape: Number line marked point; identify decimal

standards.text: Represent a fraction a/b as a sum of fractions 1/b, where a and b are whole numbers and b > 0, including when a > b.
standards.typical_question_shape: Decompose a fraction into unit fractions

standards.text: Decompose a fraction in more than one way into a sum of fractions with the same denominator using concrete and pictorial models and recording results with symbolic representations.
standards.typical_question_shape: Fraction (e.g., 5/8); pick valid decomposition (3/8 + 2/8)

standards.text: Determine if two given fractions are equivalent using a variety of methods.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two fractions; identify whether equivalent

standards.text: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators and represent the comparison using the symbols >, =, or <.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two fractions different denoms; pick the comparison

standards.text: Represent and solve addition and subtraction of fractions with equal denominators using objects and pictorial models that build to the number line and properties of operations.
standards.typical_question_shape: Add/subtract fractions same denominator (no LCD needed)

standards.text: Add and subtract whole numbers and decimals to the hundredths place using the standard algorithm.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multi-digit add/subtract with decimals; pure computation or word problem

standards.text: Determine products of a number and 10 or 100 using properties of operations and place-value understandings.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multiply by 10 or 100 mentally; identify product

standards.text: Use strategies and algorithms, including the standard algorithm, to multiply up to a four-digit number by a one-digit number and to multiply a two-digit number by a two-digit number.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multi-digit multiplication; word problem or pure computation

standards.text: Use strategies and algorithms, including the standard algorithm, to divide up to a four-digit dividend by a one-digit divisor.
standards.typical_question_shape: Division up to 4-digit ÷ 1-digit; word problem or pure

standards.text: Solve with fluency one- and two-step problems involving multiplication and division, including interpreting remainders.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multi-step word problem with multiplication/division; remainder interpretation common

standards.text: Represent multi-step problems involving the four operations with whole numbers using strip diagrams and equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem; pick the equation with variable

standards.text: Represent problems using an input-output table and numerical expressions to generate a number pattern that follows a given rule.
standards.typical_question_shape: Input-output table with one missing entry; apply rule

standards.text: Use models to determine the formulas for the perimeter of a rectangle (l + w + l + w or 2l + 2w), including the special form for perimeter of a square (4s) and the area of a rectangle (l x w).
standards.typical_question_shape: Rectangle dimensions; pick formula or compute perimeter/area

standards.text: Solve problems related to perimeter and area of rectangles where dimensions are whole numbers.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem with perimeter or area

standards.text: Identify points, lines, line segments, rays, angles, and perpendicular and parallel lines.
standards.typical_question_shape: Diagram with labeled features; identify named element

standards.text: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size.
standards.typical_question_shape: Shape image; pick category (trapezoid, parallelogram, etc.)

standards.text: Determine the approximate measures of angles in degrees to the nearest whole number using a protractor.
standards.typical_question_shape: Protractor image with marked angle; read measure

standards.text: Identify relative sizes of measurement units within the customary and metric systems.
standards.typical_question_shape: Pick larger unit (cm vs m, oz vs lb)

standards.text: Convert measurements within the same measurement system, customary or metric, from a smaller unit into a larger unit or a larger unit into a smaller unit when given other equivalent measures represented in a table.
standards.typical_question_shape: Conversion table given; convert between units

standards.text: Solve problems that deal with measurements of length, intervals of time, liquid volumes, mass, and money using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem requiring measurement + arithmetic

standards.text: Represent data on a frequency table, dot plot, or stem-and-leaf plot marked with whole numbers and fractions.
standards.typical_question_shape: Data set; identify which graphical form represents it

standards.text: Solve one- and two-step problems using data in whole number, decimal, and fraction form in a frequency table, dot plot, or stem-and-leaf plot.
standards.typical_question_shape: Stem-and-leaf or dot plot; multi-step question (sum, range, mode)

standards.text: Distinguish between fixed and variable expenses.
standards.typical_question_shape: Scenario; classify expense as fixed or variable

standards.text: Calculate profit in a given situation.
standards.typical_question_shape: Revenue and expenses given; compute profit

standards.text: Describe the basic purpose of financial institutions, including keeping money safe, borrowing money, and lending.
standards.typical_question_shape: Identify a function of a bank

standards.text: Represent the value of the digit in decimals through the thousandths using expanded notation and numerals.
standards.typical_question_shape: Decimal to thousandths; identify value of a specific digit

standards.text: Compare and order two decimals to thousandths and represent comparisons using the symbols >, <, or =.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multiple decimals; pick correct ordering

standards.text: Round decimals to tenths or hundredths.
standards.typical_question_shape: Decimal given; round to specified place

standards.text: Estimate to determine solutions to mathematical and real-world problems involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem; pick best estimate

standards.text: Multiply with fluency a three-digit number by a two-digit number using the standard algorithm.
standards.typical_question_shape: 3-digit × 2-digit multiplication

standards.text: Solve with proficiency for quotients of up to a four-digit dividend by a two-digit divisor using strategies and the standard algorithm.
standards.typical_question_shape: Long division; quotient may have remainder

standards.text: Solve for products of decimals to the hundredths, including situations involving money, using strategies based on place-value understandings, properties of operations, and the relationship to the multiplication of whole numbers.
standards.typical_question_shape: Decimal multiplication; often money word problem

standards.text: Represent quotients of decimals to the hundredths, up to four-digit dividends and two-digit whole-number divisors, using objects and pictorial models, including area models.
standards.typical_question_shape: Decimal division setup; identify model or solve

standards.text: Represent and solve addition and subtraction of fractions with unequal denominators referring to the same whole using objects and pictorial models and properties of operations.
standards.typical_question_shape: Add/subtract fractions DIFFERENT denominators (LCD required)

standards.text: Represent and solve multiplication of a whole number and a fraction that refers to the same whole using objects and pictorial models, including area models.
standards.typical_question_shape: Whole × fraction; word problem or area model

standards.text: Add and subtract positive rational numbers fluently.
standards.typical_question_shape: Mixed-number / decimal / fraction addition or subtraction

standards.text: Divide whole numbers by unit fractions and unit fractions by whole numbers.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem with division by unit fraction (e.g., 'how many 1/4 cups in 3 cups')

standards.text: Identify prime and composite numbers.
standards.typical_question_shape: List of numbers; pick prime or composite ones

standards.text: Represent and solve multi-step problems involving the four operations with whole numbers using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multi-step word problem; pick equation with variable

standards.text: Generate a numerical pattern when given a rule in the form y = ax or y = x + a and graph.
standards.typical_question_shape: Rule given; complete table or pick graph

standards.text: Simplify numerical expressions that do not involve exponents, including up to two levels of grouping.
standards.typical_question_shape: Expression with parentheses; apply order of operations

standards.text: Represent and solve problems related to perimeter and/or area and related to volume.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem with rectangular prism volume or composite area

standards.text: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy of sets and subsets using graphic organizers based on their attributes and properties.
standards.typical_question_shape: Hierarchy diagram (e.g., quadrilaterals → parallelograms → rectangles); identify category

standards.text: Recognize a cube with side length of one unit as a unit cube having one cubic unit of volume and the volume of a three-dimensional figure as the number of unit cubes (n cubic units) needed to fill it with no gaps or overlaps if possible.
standards.typical_question_shape: Image of stacked unit cubes; count volume

standards.text: Determine the volume of a rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths in problems related to the number of layers times the number of unit cubes in the area of the base.
standards.typical_question_shape: Rectangular prism dimensions; compute volume = l × w × h

standards.text: Solve problems by calculating conversions within a measurement system, customary or metric.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem requiring unit conversion (e.g., feet to inches)

standards.text: Describe the key attributes of the coordinate plane, including perpendicular number lines (axes) where the intersection (origin) of the two lines coincides with zero on each number line and the given point (0, 0); the x-coordinate, the first number in an ordered pair, indicates movement parallel to the x-axis starting at the origin; and the y-coordinate, the second number, indicates movement parallel to the y-axis starting at the origin.
standards.typical_question_shape: Coordinate-plane diagram; identify axes/origin

standards.text: Describe the process for graphing ordered pairs of numbers in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane.
standards.typical_question_shape: Plot or identify a coordinate point

standards.text: Represent categorical data with bar graphs or frequency tables and numerical data, including data sets of measurements in fractions or decimals, with dot plots or stem-and-leaf plots.
standards.typical_question_shape: Data; pick correct graph type

standards.text: Represent discrete paired data on a scatterplot.
standards.typical_question_shape: Data table; identify scatterplot

standards.text: Solve one- and two-step problems using data from a frequency table, dot plot, bar graph, stem-and-leaf plot, or scatterplot.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multi-step graph reading and computation

standards.text: Define income tax, payroll tax, sales tax, and property tax.
standards.typical_question_shape: Scenario; identify tax type

standards.text: Balance a simple budget.
standards.typical_question_shape: Income + expenses given; compute remaining

standards.text: Classify whole numbers, integers, and rational numbers using a visual representation such as a Venn diagram to describe relationships between sets of numbers.
standards.typical_question_shape: Venn diagram; classify a number

standards.text: Identify a number, its opposite, and its absolute value.
standards.typical_question_shape: Direct: opposite of -7? |−12|?

standards.text: Locate, compare, and order integers and rational numbers using a number line.
standards.typical_question_shape: Number line; place or compare integers/fractions/decimals

standards.text: Order a set of rational numbers arising from mathematical and real-world contexts.
standards.typical_question_shape: Mixed integers + fractions + decimals; pick ordering

standards.text: Extend representations for division to include fraction notation such as a/b represents the same number as a ÷ b where b ≠ 0.
standards.typical_question_shape: Identify equivalent forms (3/4 = 3 ÷ 4)

standards.text: Recognize that dividing by a rational number and multiplying by its reciprocal result in equivalent values.
standards.typical_question_shape: Identify equivalent expression involving reciprocal

standards.text: Determine, with and without computation, whether a quantity is increased or decreased when multiplied by a fraction, including values greater than or less than one.
standards.typical_question_shape: Conceptual: 'multiplying by 2/3 makes the result _____' (smaller/larger/same)

standards.text: Represent integer operations with concrete models and connect the actions with the models to standardized algorithms.
standards.typical_question_shape: Integer addition or subtraction; chip model or word problem

standards.text: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide integers fluently.
standards.typical_question_shape: Direct integer operation, e.g., −8 × 3 or 12 ÷ (−4)

standards.text: Multiply and divide positive rational numbers fluently.
standards.typical_question_shape: Decimal × decimal, fraction × fraction, mixed numbers

standards.text: Compare two rules verbally, numerically, graphically, and symbolically in the form of y = ax or y = x + a in order to differentiate between additive and multiplicative relationships.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two tables/graphs; identify which is additive vs multiplicative

standards.text: Apply qualitative and quantitative reasoning to solve prediction and comparison of real-world problems involving ratios and rates.
standards.typical_question_shape: Ratio word problem; predict outcome

standards.text: Give examples of ratios as multiplicative comparisons of two quantities describing the same attribute.
standards.typical_question_shape: Real scenario; pick the ratio that describes it

standards.text: Represent ratios and percents with concrete models, fractions, and decimals.
standards.typical_question_shape: Equivalent forms of percent (e.g., 25% = 1/4 = 0.25)

standards.text: Generate equivalent forms of fractions, decimals, and percents using real-world problems, including problems that involve money.
standards.typical_question_shape: Money word problem requiring percent ↔ decimal ↔ fraction

standards.text: Represent mathematical and real-world problems involving ratios and rates using scale factors, tables, graphs, and proportions.
standards.typical_question_shape: Recipe scaling, map scale, or unit-rate word problem

standards.text: Solve real-world problems to find the whole given a part and the percent, to find the part given the whole and the percent, and to find the percent given the part and the whole, including the use of concrete and pictorial models.
standards.typical_question_shape: Percent word problem (find part, whole, or percent)

standards.text: Use equivalent fractions, decimals, and percents to show equal parts of the same whole.
standards.typical_question_shape: Equivalent forms; pick the matching set

standards.text: Identify independent and dependent quantities from tables and graphs.
standards.typical_question_shape: Table/graph; identify which variable is independent

standards.text: Represent a given situation using verbal descriptions, tables, graphs, and equations in the form y = kx or y = x + b.
standards.typical_question_shape: Scenario; pick the matching equation/table/graph

standards.text: Generate equivalent numerical expressions using order of operations, including whole-number exponents and prime factorization.
standards.typical_question_shape: Order of operations with exponents

standards.text: Distinguish between expressions and equations verbally, numerically, and algebraically.
standards.typical_question_shape: Identify which is expression vs equation

standards.text: Determine if two expressions are equivalent using concrete models, pictorial models, and algebraic representations.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two algebraic expressions; identify whether equivalent

standards.text: Extend previous knowledge of triangles and their properties to include the sum of angles of a triangle, the relationship between the lengths of sides and measures of angles in a triangle, and determining when three lengths form a triangle.
standards.typical_question_shape: Triangle angle sum to find missing angle; triangle inequality

standards.text: Model area formulas for parallelograms, trapezoids, and triangles by decomposing and rearranging parts of these shapes.
standards.typical_question_shape: Area of triangle, trapezoid, parallelogram

standards.text: Determine solutions for problems involving the area of rectangles, parallelograms, trapezoids, and triangles and volume of right rectangular prisms where dimensions are positive rational numbers.
standards.typical_question_shape: Area or volume word problem with non-integer dimensions

standards.text: Summarize numeric data with numerical summaries, including the mean and median (measures of center) and the range and interquartile range (IQR) (measures of spread), and use these summaries to describe the center, spread, and shape of the data distribution.
standards.typical_question_shape: Data set; compute mean/median/range

standards.text: Represent numeric data graphically, including dot plots, stem-and-leaf plots, histograms, and box plots.
standards.typical_question_shape: Data; pick the matching plot

standards.text: Compare features and costs of a checking account and a debit card offered by different local financial institutions.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two account options; compare fees/features

standards.text: Balance a check register that includes deposits, withdrawals, and transfers.
standards.typical_question_shape: Check register; compute ending balance

standards.text: Extend previous knowledge of sets and subsets using a visual representation to describe relationships between sets of rational numbers.
standards.typical_question_shape: Venn or hierarchy diagram of number sets

standards.text: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers fluently.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multi-step rational-number arithmetic. Avoid bare 24÷6-type single-step (per CLAUDE.md §32 cognitive-demand spec)

standards.text: Apply and extend previous understandings of operations to solve problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of rational numbers.
standards.typical_question_shape: Multi-step word problem with rational numbers

standards.text: Represent constant rates of change in mathematical and real-world problems given pictorial, tabular, verbal, numeric, graphical, and algebraic representations, including d = rt.
standards.typical_question_shape: Distance/rate/time word problem

standards.text: Calculate unit rates from rates in mathematical and real-world problems.
standards.typical_question_shape: Rate given; compute unit rate (e.g., 240 mi in 4 hr → mph)

standards.text: Solve problems involving ratios, rates, and percents, including multi-step problems involving percent increase and percent decrease, and financial literacy problems.
standards.typical_question_shape: Tip, discount, tax, percent change word problem

standards.text: Convert between measurement systems, including the use of proportions and the use of unit rates.
standards.typical_question_shape: Conversion (e.g., miles to km) using unit rate

standards.text: Generalize the critical attributes of similarity, including ratios within and between similar shapes.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two similar figures; find missing side using ratio

standards.text: Solve mathematical and real-world problems involving similar shape and scale drawings.
standards.typical_question_shape: Scale drawing; compute actual dimension

standards.text: Represent sample spaces for simple and compound events using lists and tree diagrams.
standards.typical_question_shape: Sample space (e.g., 2 coins); identify outcomes

standards.text: Make predictions and determine solutions using experimental data for simple and compound events.
standards.typical_question_shape: Experimental probability from data table

standards.text: Make predictions and determine solutions using theoretical probability for simple and compound events.
standards.typical_question_shape: Theoretical probability of compound event (P(A and B), P(A or B))

standards.text: Determine experimental and theoretical probabilities related to simple and compound events using data and sample spaces.
standards.typical_question_shape: Compare experimental vs theoretical probability

standards.text: Represent linear relationships using verbal descriptions, tables, graphs, and equations that simplify to the form y = mx + b.
standards.typical_question_shape: Scenario; pick equation in y=mx+b form

standards.text: Model the relationship between the volume of a rectangular prism and a rectangular pyramid having both congruent bases and heights and connect that relationship to the formulas.
standards.typical_question_shape: Volume comparison; pyramid = 1/3 × prism

standards.text: Solve problems involving the volume of rectangular prisms, triangular prisms, rectangular pyramids, and triangular pyramids.
standards.typical_question_shape: Volume word problem with prism or pyramid

standards.text: Determine the circumference and area of circles.
standards.typical_question_shape: Circle problem with given radius/diameter; π

standards.text: Determine the area of composite figures containing combinations of rectangles, squares, parallelograms, trapezoids, triangles, semicircles, and quarter circles.
standards.typical_question_shape: Composite figure; sum areas of parts

standards.text: Write one-variable, two-step equations and inequalities to represent constraints or conditions within problems.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem; pick equation/inequality

standards.text: Model and solve one-variable, two-step equations and inequalities.
standards.typical_question_shape: Solve 2x + 3 = 11 or 5x − 2 ≥ 18

standards.text: Determine if the given value(s) make(s) one-variable, two-step equations and inequalities true.
standards.typical_question_shape: Substitute value; check truth

standards.text: Compare two groups of numeric data using comparative dot plots or box plots by comparing their shapes, centers, and spreads.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two box plots side by side; compare medians/spreads

standards.text: Use data from a random sample to make inferences about a population.
standards.typical_question_shape: Sample statistics; estimate population parameter

standards.text: Calculate the sales tax for a given purchase and calculate income tax for earned wages.
standards.typical_question_shape: Compute sales tax on purchase OR income tax on wages

standards.text: Calculate and compare simple interest and compound interest earnings.
standards.typical_question_shape: Compare simple vs compound interest over N years

standards.text: Extend previous knowledge of sets and subsets using a visual representation to describe relationships between sets of real numbers.
standards.typical_question_shape: Hierarchy of real numbers (rational, irrational, integers)

standards.text: Approximate the value of an irrational number, including π and square roots of numbers less than 225, and locate that rational number approximation on a number line.
standards.typical_question_shape: Place √50 between consecutive integers

standards.text: Convert between standard decimal notation and scientific notation.
standards.typical_question_shape: Decimal ↔ scientific notation

standards.text: Order a set of real numbers arising from mathematical and real-world contexts.
standards.typical_question_shape: Mixed real numbers (rationals + irrationals); pick correct ordering

standards.text: Generalize that the ratio of corresponding sides of similar shapes is proportional, including a shape and its dilation.
standards.typical_question_shape: Dilated figure; find scale factor or missing side

standards.text: Compare and contrast the attributes of a shape and its dilation(s) on a coordinate plane.
standards.typical_question_shape: Pre-image vs image after dilation; identify scale factor

standards.text: Use similar right triangles to develop an understanding that slope, m, given as the rate comparing the change in y-values to the change in x-values, (y2 − y1)/(x2 − x1), is the same for any two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) on the same line.
standards.typical_question_shape: Slope from two points or similar-triangle setup

standards.text: Graph proportional relationships, interpreting the unit rate as the slope of the line that models the relationship.
standards.typical_question_shape: Linear graph through origin; identify unit rate / slope

standards.text: Use data from a table or graph to determine the rate of change or slope and y-intercept in mathematical and real-world problems.
standards.typical_question_shape: Table or graph; identify slope AND y-intercept

standards.text: Represent linear proportional situations with tables, graphs, and equations in the form of y = kx.
standards.typical_question_shape: Proportional situation; pick the y=kx equation

standards.text: Represent linear non-proportional situations with tables, graphs, and equations in the form of y = mx + b, where b ≠ 0.
standards.typical_question_shape: Non-proportional linear situation; pick y=mx+b

standards.text: Identify functions using sets of ordered pairs, tables, mappings, and graphs.
standards.typical_question_shape: Relation given; identify whether function (vertical line test or each x maps to single y)

standards.text: Write an equation in the form y = mx + b to model a linear relationship between two quantities using verbal, numerical, tabular, and graphical representations.
standards.typical_question_shape: Verbal or graphical scenario; write equation y=mx+b

standards.text: Use the Pythagorean Theorem and its converse to solve problems.
standards.typical_question_shape: Right triangle; find hypotenuse or leg using a²+b²=c²

standards.text: Use models and diagrams to explain the Pythagorean theorem.
standards.typical_question_shape: Pythagorean theorem applied to area-of-squares model

standards.text: Use the Pythagorean Theorem and its converse to solve problems involving distance.
standards.typical_question_shape: Coordinate distance; ladder/triangle word problem

standards.text: Determine the volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.
standards.typical_question_shape: Volume of cylinder/cone/sphere with given radius and height

standards.text: Write one-variable equations or inequalities with variables on both sides that represent problems using rational number coefficients and constants.
standards.typical_question_shape: Word problem; pick equation with variable on both sides

standards.text: Model and solve one-variable equations with variables on both sides of the equal sign that represent mathematical and real-world problems using rational number coefficients and constants.
standards.typical_question_shape: Solve 3x + 5 = 2x − 7 type equations

standards.text: Generalize the properties of orientation and congruence of rotations, reflections, translations, and dilations of two-dimensional shapes on a coordinate plane.
standards.typical_question_shape: Pre-image; identify image after specified transformation

standards.text: Construct a scatterplot and describe the observed data to address questions of association such as linear, non-linear, and no association between bivariate data.
standards.typical_question_shape: Scatterplot; identify association type

standards.text: Solve real-world problems comparing how interest rate and loan length affect the cost of credit.
standards.typical_question_shape: Compare loan total cost across rate/term combinations

standards.text: Calculate and compare simple interest and compound interest earnings.
standards.typical_question_shape: Simple vs compound interest over N years

algebra_1.course_note: Algebra I is taught in 8th or 9th grade in Texas; STAAR EOC required for graduation.

standards.text: Determine the domain and range of a linear function in mathematical problems; determine reasonable domain and range values for real-world situations, both continuous and discrete; and represent domain and range using inequalities.
standards.typical_question_shape: Function given; identify domain/range

standards.text: Write linear equations in two variables in various forms, including y = mx + b, Ax + By = C, and y − y1 = m(x − x1), given one point and the slope and given two points.
standards.typical_question_shape: Two points or point+slope; write equation

standards.text: Write and solve equations involving direct variation.
standards.typical_question_shape: Direct variation y=kx; find k from a point

standards.text: Write linear inequalities in two variables given a table of values, a graph, and a verbal description.
standards.typical_question_shape: Inequality from graph (with shaded region) or word problem

standards.text: Determine the slope of a line given a table of values, a graph, two points on the line, and an equation written in various forms, including y = mx + b, Ax + By = C, and y − y1 = m(x − x1).
standards.typical_question_shape: Slope from any of: table, graph, two points, equation form

standards.text: Calculate the rate of change of a linear function represented tabularly, graphically, or algebraically in context of mathematical and real-world problems.
standards.typical_question_shape: Rate of change in real context (e.g., $/hour, miles/gal)

standards.text: Graph linear functions on the coordinate plane and identify key features, including x-intercept, y-intercept, zeros, and slope, in mathematical and real-world problems.
standards.typical_question_shape: Graph or interpret linear function with intercepts/slope

standards.text: Solve linear equations in one variable, including those for which the application of the distributive property is necessary and for which variables are included on both sides.
standards.typical_question_shape: Linear equation requiring distribution + variables both sides

standards.text: Solve linear inequalities in one variable, including those for which the application of the distributive property is necessary and for which variables are included on both sides.
standards.typical_question_shape: Solve inequality; remember to flip on multiply/divide by negative

standards.text: Solve systems of two linear equations with two variables for mathematical and real-world problems.
standards.typical_question_shape: System of equations; substitution or elimination

standards.text: Determine the domain and range of quadratic functions and represent the domain and range using inequalities.
standards.typical_question_shape: Quadratic given; identify domain/range (parabola opens up/down)

standards.text: Write equations of quadratic functions given the vertex and another point on the graph, write the equation in vertex form (f(x) = a(x − h)² + k), and rewrite the equation from vertex form to standard form (f(x) = ax² + bx + c).
standards.typical_question_shape: Vertex form ↔ standard form; or write from vertex + point

standards.text: Write quadratic functions when given real solutions and graphs of their related equations.
standards.typical_question_shape: Roots given; write quadratic in factored form

standards.text: Graph quadratic functions on the coordinate plane and use the graph to identify key attributes, if possible, including x-intercept, y-intercept, zeros, maximum value, minimum value, vertex, and the equation of the axis of symmetry.
standards.typical_question_shape: Parabola graph; identify vertex/zeros/axis of symmetry

standards.text: Describe the relationship between the linear factors of quadratic expressions and the zeros of their associated quadratic functions.
standards.typical_question_shape: Factored form (x−p)(x−q); identify zeros

standards.text: Determine the effects on the graph of the parent function f(x) = x² when f(x) is replaced by af(x), f(x) + d, f(bx), or f(x − c) for specific positive and negative real values of a, b, c, and d.
standards.typical_question_shape: Transformation of parabola; identify shift/stretch/reflect

standards.text: Solve quadratic equations having real solutions by factoring, taking square roots, completing the square, and applying the quadratic formula.
standards.typical_question_shape: Solve quadratic by factoring or quadratic formula

standards.text: Add and subtract polynomials of degree one and degree two.
standards.typical_question_shape: Combine like terms in polynomial sum/difference

standards.text: Multiply polynomials of degree one and degree two.
standards.typical_question_shape: (x+a)(x+b) expansion (FOIL)

standards.text: Factor, if possible, trinomials with real factors in the form ax² + bx + c, including perfect square trinomials of degree two.
standards.typical_question_shape: Factor trinomial; identify factors

standards.text: Simplify numerical radical expressions involving square roots.
standards.typical_question_shape: Simplify √48 = 4√3 type

standards.text: Simplify numeric and algebraic expressions using the laws of exponents, including integral and rational exponents.
standards.typical_question_shape: Apply exponent rules: x³·x⁵, (x²)³, etc.

standards.text: Decide whether relations represented verbally, tabularly, graphically, and symbolically define a function.
standards.typical_question_shape: Identify function vs not

standards.text: Evaluate functions, expressed in function notation, given one or more elements in their domains.
standards.typical_question_shape: f(x) = ...; find f(3)

standards.text: Identify terms of arithmetic and geometric sequences when the sequences are given in function form using recursive processes.
standards.typical_question_shape: Recursive rule; find next term or specific term
